news.title: ₹60 Crore mega residential deal: DGS Group promoters Brahamdev Shukla, Sourav Shukla & Rohan Shukla snap up luxury unit at Oberoi Elysian, Goregaon East
news.para: Mumbai's luxury housing market sees a major deal. Brahamdev Shukla, Sourav Shukla, and Rohan Shukla of DGS Group have bought a ₹60 crore apartment in Oberoi Elysian, Goregaon East. This large acquisition highlights Goregaon East's growing appeal to wealthy buyers. The project is currently under construction.

news.title: Max Estates reports ₹5,305 crore pre-sales in FY26
news.para: Max Estates reported pre-sales of ₹5,305 crore in FY2026, with the March quarter contributing ₹3,392 crore. Key projects like Estate 105 in Noida and Estate 361 in Gurugram drove these strong sales. The company's net debt stood at approximately ₹174 crore as of March 2026, with a robust project pipeline valued over ₹16,000 crore.

news.title: Ahmedabad civic body puts parking onus on property owners
news.para: Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation has implemented strict new parking regulations, placing the onus on property owners to manage vehicle parking within their premises. Non-compliance risks building usage permission suspension, with owners now responsible for preventing street parking and ensuring clear, marked bays. Buildings must also remove obstructions and submit annual self-declarations regarding parking area integrity.

news.title: GCC leasing hits record 9.1 million sq ft in Q1 2026: Report
news.para: India's office market achieved its strongest first quarter ever in Q1 2026, with gross leasing hitting 20.7 million sq ft. Global Capability Centres (GCCs) drove this surge, leasing 9.1 million sq ft, with Bengaluru and Hyderabad leading as key hubs. Demand favored high-quality, green-certified, and newer assets.

news.title: PMRDA seeks clarity from Maharashtra govt on Maan-Mhalunge town planning scheme execution
news.para: PMRDA seeks state clarity on executing the Maan-Mhalunge Town Planning Scheme (TPS-1) following Mhalunge's merger with PMC. The 250-hectare project, initiated by PMRDA in 2017, is nearing implementation, with landowners urging speedy approval for planned development in the Hinjewadi-Maan-Mhalunge corridor.

news.title: Shreya Ghoshal, parents buy residential unit in Mumbai for a ₹29.70 crore
news.para: Singer Shreya Ghoshal, accompanied by her parents, has acquired a luxurious apartment in Mumbai's Godrej Trilogy, Worli, for ₹29.70 crore. The spacious property spans over 2,750 sq ft and includes three car parking spaces. The significant transaction, registered in April 2026, involved substantial stamp duty and registration charges.

news.title: Developer cannot take extra charge for PNG connections: Green Gas
news.para: Green Gas Limited has clarified that residents will not face additional charges for piped natural gas connections. This comes after a real estate developer was found to be levying extra fees. The district administration intervened following complaints from residents of Sushant Golf City, Ansal API.

news.title: Keystone Realtors' pre-sales grows 58% in Q4 FY26
news.para: Keystone Realtors announced strong financial results for FY26. Pre-sales surged by 58% year-on-year in Q4 FY26. Full-year pre-sales grew 33%. Collections also saw a 13% increase for the full year. The company successfully launched seven projects and added five new projects, significantly exceeding its guidance for both business development and launches.

news.title: Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar: CSMC to execute document verification of government housing scheme
news.para: Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar Municipal Corporation begins document verification for Ramai Awas Yojana beneficiaries from April 7. The scheme, aimed at providing housing to Scheduled Castes and Neo-Buddhists, faces past allegations of bogus beneficiaries and fund siphoning. Former MP Imtiaz Jaleel demands investigation into past payments and recovery from alleged fraudsters.

news.title: SIT soon to investigate EWS quota violation in Nashik: Maharashtra revenue minister
news.para: The Maharashtra government is gearing up to launch a Special Investigation Team to delve into the alleged misconduct of 197 developers in Nashik, who stand accused of sidestepping the essential 20% reservation for economically disadvantaged communities. This dedicated team, consisting of one IPS and two IAS officers, represents the state's determination to uphold justice.

news.title: Greater Noida residents protest metro delay after DPR rejection
news.para: Residents of Greater Noida West protested the Centre's rejection of the Metro extension detailed project report. They highlighted daily traffic congestion and the lack of public transport.  Protesters demanded the government prioritize the project.  Further demonstrations are planned if action is not taken.  The project faces further delays.

news.title: Maharashtra government to fast-track land surveys with private surveyors
news.para: Maharashtra's government has approved a statewide overhaul of land survey processes, appointing licensed private surveyors and agencies to clear backlogs and accelerate development. This reform aims for accurate, time-bound, and transparent land measurement, with officials facing strict penalties for corruption. The move is expected to expedite stalled projects and boost the state's progress by ensuring prompt surveys.

news.title: Delhi development body's conversion of leashold properties to freehold halted for review
news.para: Delhi Development Authority has halted leasehold-to-freehold property conversions for three months, leaving applicants in uncertainty. The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs is reviewing the policy to rationalize rates and simplify procedures, with changes expected soon. This pause follows a previous increase in conversion charges for commercial properties.

news.title: Project 'unsafe', pay ₹4 crore to buyer: Haryana RERA to Chintels India
news.para: Haryana Rera has ordered Chintels India to pay over Rs 4 crore to a homebuyer in Chintels Paradiso due to grave construction defects. The order follows a fatal collapse in Tower D, revealing severe structural failures across the complex. Rera found the developer solely responsible for the project's unsuitability for habitation.

news.title: Goa consumer forum dismisses complaint of compensation for deficiency in service
news.para: A consumer commission dismissed a complaint against a proprietor for failing to hand over plots, citing a lack of payment receipts as crucial evidence. The majority ruled that without proof of payment, the claim could not be substantiated.

news.title: GIS survey of Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar development body areas begin
news.para: A Geographic Information System (GIS) survey has commenced across 311 villages under the Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar Metropolitan Regional Development Authority (CSMRDA). The pilot phase covers five villages, with a private agency collecting data and conducting site inspections. This initiative aims to ensure comprehensive future development and provide essential infrastructure to the region, including the burgeoning AURIC industrial hub.

news.title: Punjab government extends one time settlement for taxpayers till May 31
news.para: Punjab government has extended the One Time Settlement scheme for outstanding dues. Taxpayers now have until May 31, 2026, to apply. This offers a final chance to resolve pending dues and get financial relief. The government has already waived significant amounts. Properties of defaulters will face auction if action is not taken. This extension aims to increase participation and revenue.

news.title: BMC raises concerns over lapses in action against dangerous buildings in Mumbai
news.para: Mumbai civic body flags serious procedural lapses in actions against dangerous buildings. Ward officials are warned of disciplinary and legal action for failing to follow mandated digital recording procedures and development rules. Non-compliance in processing redevelopment documents could lead to financial losses for the BMC, with strict adherence to SOPs now enforced.
